title: Endurance Training Program
description: Comprehensive endurance program to improve cardiovascular fitness, build stamina, and enhance aerobic capacity for distance activities.
overview: This endurance program is structured around the principles of periodization, building aerobic base, threshold work, and VO2 max development. Suitable for runners, cyclists, and anyone wanting to build cardiovascular endurance. Expected improvements: 15-20% increase in VO2 max over 12 weeks.

weekly_schedule.exercises: Warm-up 10min + 8x (3min hard / 2min easy) + 5min cool-down. Sport: Running, cycling, or rowing
Squats (3x8), Deadlifts (3x5), Bulgarian Squats (3x8), Core work (3x10), Plyometrics
Long easy run/bike/swim at conversational pace. Distance increases each week by 5-10%
Warm-up 10min + 20-30min at hard but sustainable pace + 5min cool-down
Easy walk, swim, or light cycling with stretching and foam rolling
Very long easy to moderate pace session. Gradually build time on feet

nutrition.content: 2-3 cups oatmeal + 1 banana + 1 tbsp honey + 30g whey protein
1-2 bananas or sports drink (30-60g carbs) + 200ml water
Sports drink, energy gels, or energy bars (30-60g carbs per hour)
Chocolate milk or 50g whey + 60g simple carbs (dates, rice)
200g salmon + 250g sweet potato + 200g broccoli + olive oil

tips: Build mileage gradually - no more than 10% increase per week
Long run should be easy and at conversational pace - slower than you think
Include one intense session per week for VO2 max improvement
Invest in proper footwear for your sport
Track all workouts and RPE (Rate of Perceived Exertion)
Stay hydrated constantly - drink water throughout the day
Use electrolytes during workouts lasting over 60 minutes
Include proper warm-up (10min) and cool-down (5min) for every session
Perform dynamic stretching before and static stretching after
Get 7-9 hours of sleep for optimal recovery
Don't do hard workouts on consecutive days
Include mobility and foam rolling 3x per week
Listen to your body - take extra rest if needed to prevent injury
